scope: Sprint 8 regression review (iter-2 patches) — behavioral-spec.md §15 (REQ-IPC-001..020, EC-IPC-001..014) and verification-architecture.md §10 (PROP-IPC-001..023, §10.2.1 cover set, §10.5 2-step pipeline)

evidence.description: FIND-073 RESOLVED in iter-2 by REQ-IPC-014 rewrite: focusedBlockId is now mandated as null for Sprint 8 with explicit deferral note 'Sprint 8's Rust handler shall NOT attempt to compute a Block.id from raw markdown bytes — block parsing remains out-of-scope per §15.4'. The contradiction with §15.4 is removed.

FIND-075 RESOLVED in iter-2 by REQ-IPC-005 positive-form rewrite: 'shall contain exactly the keys status, currentNoteId, isNoteEmpty, and (when the optional blocks field is Some) blocks — and no other keys.' Mirrors REQ-IPC-004/006/007 form; PROP-IPC-006 enforces equality.

FIND-076 RESOLVED in iter-2 by REQ-IPC-007 rewrite: positive enumeration now includes 'isNoteEmpty' explicitly, matching PROP-IPC-005 and TS types.ts. REQ-IPC-006 (line 1212) similarly includes isNoteEmpty in the switching variant key set.

FIND-080 RESOLVED in iter-2 by REQ-IPC-017 rewrite: now explicitly documents 'Known Sprint-8 regression vs Sprint-7 RD-018' for pendingNextFocus: null and defers the fix to Sprint 9 / ui-feed-list-actions Sprint 4. The silent demotion is gone.

FIND-078 RESOLVED in iter-2 by PROP-IPC-011 rewrite + §10.5 2-step pipeline: fixtures are now generated by 'cargo test print_wire_fixtures' (which writes promptnotes/src-tauri/tests/fixtures/wire-fixtures.json from §10.2.1 cover set) and consumed by vitest. Drift detection closed: a Rust field-name change regenerates the fixture, breaks the TS test on the next CI run.

FIND-082 RESOLVED in iter-2 by PROP-IPC-013..017 additions: per-handler integration PROPs now exist for discard_current_session (013), cancel_switch (014), request_new_note (015), trigger_idle_save/blur_save/retry_save Ok+Err (016), and select_past_note (017) using Tauri mocks::mock_app(). Every emit-site listed in REQ-IPC-013..018 is now individually verified.

FIND-084 RESOLVED in iter-2 by §10.2.1 explicit 14-fixture cover-set table (F01..F14): hand-enumerated, includes blocks=Some([nonEmpty]) on F04 (Editing, 4 BlockTypeDto variants) and F14 (SaveFailed, 2 BlockTypeDto variants), drops 'where applicable' qualifier. The 'canonical' label commits the table to revision-control discipline.

FIND-079 RESOLVED in iter-2 by EC-IPC-011..014 additions: (a) EC-IPC-011 covers select_past_note for missing noteId with body='' fallback; (b) EC-IPC-012 covers cancel_switch from switching (not save-failed) with TS-side fallback documented; (c) EC-IPC-013 covers whitespace-only body and explicit divergence from NoteOps.isEmpty(); (d) EC-IPC-014 covers emit-before-subscribe race as an EditorPanel.svelte mount-order obligation. All four gaps from FIND-079 are now first-class ECs.

FIND-074 RESOLVED in iter-2 by REQ-IPC-015 rewrite: focus restoration is explicitly delegated to TS-side EditorPanel local state (RD-022 / §3.6a), not the reducer. The new sentence 'PROP-EDIT-040's idle-default fallback ... is NOT relied upon for cancel-switch focus restoration' closes the previous unspecified mechanism. PROP-IPC-023 verifies reducer robustness on this transition (focusedBlockId: null is legitimate).

FIND-077 RESOLVED in iter-2 by §15.5 explicit serde-annotation prohibition: 'The Rust enum-variant fields Editing::focused_block_id, SaveFailed::prior_focused_block_id, and SaveFailed::pending_next_focus MUST NOT carry #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]. ... Only ... blocks ... and SaveErrorDto::reason carry the skip annotation.' PROP-IPC-020 grep audit enforces this at CI. Allow-list is well-scoped.

FIND-081 RESOLVED in iter-2 by BlockTypeDto enum specification in §15.5 + PROP-IPC-018 (compile-time exhaustiveness) + PROP-IPC-019 (round-trip on 9 valid + 3 invalid values). Round-trip validation is enforced at the Rust serde layer.

FIND-086 RESOLVED in iter-2 by PROP-IPC-022: TS test asserts 'new Set(Object.keys(state)) equals the variant's expected key-set' using Set EQUALITY (not subset). Extra-keys drift is detected at runtime. PROP-IPC-011 now explicitly references this equality semantics.

FIND-083 RESOLVED in iter-2 by §15.5 body-delivery routing pin: 'past-note bodies for the editor are delivered via feed_state_changed.note_metadata[noteId].body (already populated by feed.rs::scan_vault_feed)'. Cross-feature dependency on ui-feed-list-actions is named explicitly; §15.6 smoke test no longer ambiguous.

FIND-085 RESOLVED in iter-2 by §15.5 helper-API pin: SINGULAR form 'pub fn make_editing_state_changed_payload(state: &EditingSessionStateDto) -> serde_json::Value' is mandated. Per-variant constructors are explicitly NOT used. PROP-IPC-009 / PROP-IPC-012 align on the singular form.

summary.narrative:
Iter-2 substantially addressed iter-1's 14 findings. 13 of 14 are cleanly resolved by concrete spec/architecture deltas: REQ-IPC-004..007 rewritten in positive exact-key-set form (FIND-075/076); REQ-IPC-014 contradiction with §15.4 removed by mandating focusedBlockId: null and naming the deferral target (FIND-073); REQ-IPC-015 cancel-switch focus restoration explicitly delegated to TS EditorPanel local state with PROP-IPC-023 verifying reducer robustness (FIND-074); REQ-IPC-017 silent regression promoted to documented known-limitation deferred to Sprint 9 (FIND-080); §15.5 forbids skip_serializing_if on focus fields with PROP-IPC-020 grep audit (FIND-077); BlockTypeDto enum + PROP-IPC-018/019 close the type-validation gap (FIND-081); PROP-IPC-013..017 add per-handler integration tests with Tauri mocks (FIND-082); body delivery routed via feed_state_changed.note_metadata, pinning the cross-feature dependency (FIND-083); single helper API pinned (FIND-085); §10.2.1 14-fixture cover-set table is canonical and exercises blocks=Some([nonEmpty]) (FIND-084); §10.5 2-step pipeline (cargo test print_wire_fixtures → vitest) closes the drift loop (FIND-078); EC-IPC-011..014 cover the 4 missing edge cases (FIND-079); PROP-IPC-022 enforces Set EQUALITY for extra-keys detection (FIND-086).

One new finding introduced by the patches blocks PASS: REQ-IPC-009 (line 1218) still describes the Rust source as 'block_type: String', directly contradicting §15.5's BlockTypeDto enum mandate (line 1272) and PROP-IPC-018/019. This is a single-sentence stale phrasing — the wire-shape claim ({ id, type, content }) is correct, only the Rust field-type description is wrong. Trivial 1-line edit. Strict mode does not tolerate this internal contradiction within the same sprint's spec, so overall verdict is FAIL.

All other dimensions are PASS. Recommendation: route FIND-087 to Phase 1a for a one-line REQ-IPC-009 amendment, then iter-3 should converge cleanly.
